notes: Lesson 23 Part 2 (act 136), '0/1 Knapsack — a rolling row swept descending', 79.54s, 6 cues. Capacity W=7, items X(w3,v4) Y(w4,v5). dp init [0]*8 -> after X (descending w7..3) [0,0,0,4,4,4,4,4] -> after Y (descending w7..4) [0,0,0,4,5,5,5,9]; dp[7]=9 = X(4)+Y(5), weight 3+4=7. Uses arrays.py (value_row/recolor_cell/complexity) + new dp.py (item_card, sweep_arrow, choice_pair). One iteration: authored + manim -s smoke all 6 cues READ (clean, no layout defects) + full -ql render + READ start/mid/end of every cue from muxed mp4. Per-cue checklist (dp[w]=best value within budget w, skip/take recurrence dp[w]=max(dp[w], dp[w-wt]+val) correct, 2D->1D rolling-array collapse, descending sweep keeps dp[w-3] as pre-item value so X lands once + ascending-double-count warning, dp[7]=9 answer with both item cards emerald, ascending flip -> unbounded + O(nW)/O(W) bound, safe-area guard, chunk timing pinned): ALL PASS. Note: cue04_end frame shows cue05's title 'Flip to ascending' — the documented keyframe-seek boundary artifact (extraction at cue-end lands in the next chunk); the cue04 content (dp[7]=9) is confirmed by cue04_mid + the -s smoke last-frame.
